chapter_context.summary: David desires to build a temple for the LORD; Nathan delivers the Davidic Covenant—God promises to build David an eternal house and dynasty.

outline.text: David Desires to Build a House for the LORD
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Desires to Build a House for the LORD (7:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: The Davidic Covenant; God's Promise of an Eternal Dynasty
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Davidic Covenant; God's Promise of an Eternal Dynasty (7:4-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David's Beautiful Prayer of Thanksgiving and Praise
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Beautiful Prayer of Thanksgiving and Praise (7:18-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

key_themes.title: The Davidic Covenant
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promise of an eternal dynasty, ultimately pointing forward to the Messiah.

key_themes.title: Sin and Consequences
key_themes.description: While God offers complete forgiveness for sincere repentance, the earthly consequences of sin remain.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 7 demonstrate the grace of God in the midst of human failure?
What lessons about leadership and accountability can you apply to your own life from this chapter?

cultural_historical.title: Jerusalem as Capital
cultural_historical.text: By capturing Jerusalem, a neutral city on the border of Judah and Benjamin, David strategically united the fractured northern and southern tribes under a single capital.
